models.name: Samsung WF45T6000AW Front-Load Washer
models.notes: One of Samsung's best-selling 4.5 cu ft front-load washers. Uses Samsung's standard front-load diagnostic codes shown on the LED panel.

models.name: Samsung WF45R6100AP Front-Load Washer
models.notes: Steam-capable front-loader with Self Clean+. Shares the diagnostic code set used across Samsung's R-series front-load platform.

models.name: Samsung DVE45R6100C Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7.5 cu ft electric dryer with steam sanitize. Codes display on the front LED panel; heating element and vent codes dominate service calls.

models.name: Samsung DW80R5060US StormWash Dishwasher
models.notes: Popular StormWash model. The leak-sensor LC code is the single most common complaint on this platform — often triggered by detergent overflow rather than a real leak.

models.name: Samsung RF28R7351SG 4-Door French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: Family Hub-adjacent 28 cu ft French door model. Ice-maker and fan codes are the most frequent; codes show on the front display panel.

models.name: LG WM3900HWA Front-Load Washer
models.notes: 4.5 cu ft TurboWash 360 front-loader, one of LG's most-reviewed washers. Direct-drive motor; drain and inlet codes are the most common.

models.name: LG WM4000HBA Front-Load Washer
models.notes: Successor to the WM3900 with the same diagnostic code set. AI DD drum sensing; balance (UE) complaints drop but drain codes remain common.

models.name: LG DLEX3900W Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7.4 cu ft TurboSteam dryer. The D80/D90/D95 airflow-blockage family is by far the most common code group on this model — almost always lint restriction in the duct run.

models.name: LG LFXS26973S French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 26 cu ft smart French door model with dual ice makers. Fan and defrost-circuit codes shown via the internal display panel; linear compressor era model.

models.name: Whirlpool WTW5000DW Top-Load Washer
models.notes: High-volume 4.3 cu ft VMW-platform top loader. Lid lock and fill codes dominate; most are resolvable without parts.

models.name: Whirlpool WFW5620HW Front-Load Washer
models.notes: 4.5 cu ft front-loader with Load & Go dispenser. Drain (F9E1-family) and door-lock codes are the most common service triggers.

models.name: Whirlpool WED4815EW Electric Dryer
models.notes: Budget 7 cu ft electric dryer. Thermal fuse and heating circuit codes are the usual suspects; vent restriction is the root cause in most cases.

models.name: Whirlpool WDF520PADM Dishwasher
models.notes: Workhorse budget dishwasher. Codes are read as blink patterns on the Clean light or shown as F-E pairs on display models.

models.name: Whirlpool WRS325SDHZ Side-by-Side Refrigerator
models.notes: 25 cu ft side-by-side with in-door ice. PO (power outage) and communication codes show on the dispenser display.

models.name: GE GTW465ASNWW Top-Load Washer
models.notes: 4.5 cu ft top loader with dual-action agitator. GE top-load codes display via LED blink patterns or on-screen on lid models.

models.name: GE GDF630PSMSS Dishwasher
models.notes: Front-control dishwasher with Dry Boost. Leak and fill codes are the most common; codes show on the control panel display.

models.name: GE GNE27JYMFS French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 27 cu ft fingerprint-resistant French door model. Fan, defrost, and ice-maker codes show on the temperature display panel.

models.name: Bosch SHEM63W55N 300 Series Dishwasher
models.notes: The best-selling Bosch 300 Series platform. E15 (water in base pan) is the signature Bosch code — the anti-flood switch trips and the unit refuses to run until the base is dry.

models.name: Bosch SHPM65Z55N 500 Series Dishwasher
models.notes: 500 Series with AutoAir dry and the same diagnostic set as the 300 Series. Drain (E24/E25) and heat-pump codes are the most frequent.

models.name: Maytag MVWC465HW Top-Load Washer
models.notes: Maytag's VMW-platform top loader — mechanically the same platform as Whirlpool's WTW series, so it shares the F#E# code set.

models.name: Maytag MEDC465HW Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7 cu ft electric dryer on the Whirlpool platform. Thermal and heating-circuit codes are the usual failures; check the vent run first.

models.name: Kenmore 110-Series Washers (Whirlpool-built)
models.notes: Model numbers starting with 110. are Whirlpool-built Kenmore washers. They use Whirlpool's F-code diagnostic set: F21 slow drain, F20 fill failure, and friends.

models.name: Kenmore 796-Series Washers (LG-built)
models.notes: Model numbers starting with 796. are LG-built Kenmore (mostly Elite) washers. They use LG's two-letter code set: OE, IE, LE, UE.

models.name: Frigidaire FFCD2418US Dishwasher
models.notes: High-volume budget dishwasher. Frigidaire's i-codes (i20, i30) cover drain restriction and base-pan leak detection.

models.name: Electrolux EFLS627UTT Front-Load Washer
models.notes: Premium front-loader with SmartBoost. Electrolux E-codes display directly; drain and door-lock codes lead service data.

models.name: Samsung WF42H5000AW Front-Load Washer
models.notes: High-volume 4.2 cu ft front-loader from Samsung's H-series era. Door-boot mold complaints and water-supply (4C) codes dominate its service history.

models.name: Samsung WA50R5400AV Top-Load Washer
models.notes: 5.0 cu ft top loader with Active WaterJet. Unbalance (UE) codes are the most common complaint on this platform — usually a load distribution issue, not a part failure.

models.name: Samsung WA45T3200AW Top-Load Washer
models.notes: Budget 4.5 cu ft top loader. Simple LED display shows the standard Samsung top-load code set; fill and unbalance codes lead service data.

models.name: Samsung WF50BG8300AV Bespoke Front-Load Washer
models.notes: Bespoke 5.0 cu ft front-loader with AI OptiWash. Codes appear on the full-color display; the AP code (SmartThings access point mode) is often mistaken for an error.

models.name: Samsung WV60M9900AV FlexWash Washer
models.notes: Dual-drum FlexWash — a front-loader with a small top-load tub above. Each tub reports codes separately, so note which display shows the error before troubleshooting.

models.name: Samsung WA54R7600AV Top-Load Washer
models.notes: 5.4 cu ft top loader with built-in faucet. Shares the WA-series code set; leak (LE) and unbalance (UE) codes are the most frequent service triggers.

models.name: Samsung DVE45T6000W Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7.5 cu ft electric dryer, successor to the R-series. Heating element (HC) and clog-warning (Clg) codes are the most common; check the vent run before replacing parts.

models.name: Samsung DV42H5000EW Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7.5 cu ft H-series electric dryer. Thermistor (tE) and heating (HE) codes dominate; the heating element on this platform is a common DIY replacement.

models.name: Samsung DVE50BG8300V Bespoke Electric Dryer
models.notes: Bespoke 7.6 cu ft dryer with AI Dry. The Clg clog warning fires early and often — it is a maintenance prompt for the duct run, not a component failure.

models.name: Samsung DVG45T3200W Gas Dryer
models.notes: 7.5 cu ft gas dryer. The nG (no gas) code usually means a closed supply valve or failed igniter rather than a control fault.

models.name: Samsung DW80K5050US Dishwasher
models.notes: K-series StormWash predecessor. The LC leak-sensor code is the defining complaint — moisture in the base pan from detergent overflow trips it as readily as a real leak.

models.name: Samsung DW80B6060US StormWash+ Dishwasher
models.notes: StormWash+ with AutoRelease door. Same diagnostic family as earlier DW80 platforms; drain (5C) and leak (LC) codes lead service calls.

models.name: Samsung DW80CG4051SR Dishwasher
models.notes: Current-generation budget Samsung dishwasher. Codes show as letter-number pairs on the front display; the code set carries over from the DW80 platform.

models.name: Samsung RF263BEAESR French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: One of the best-selling French door fridges of its decade. Ice-maker freeze-up and the 22E fan code are the platform's signature issues.

models.name: Samsung RF23M8070SR Counter-Depth French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: Counter-depth 4-door Flex model. Fan codes (22E, 26E) often trace to evaporator icing — defrost the unit fully before condemning the fan motor.

models.name: Samsung RS27T5200SR Side-by-Side Refrigerator
models.notes: 27 cu ft side-by-side. Codes display on the dispenser panel; fan and defrost-sensor codes are the most common on this platform.

models.name: Samsung RF27T5201SR French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 27 cu ft 3-door French door model. The ice-maker codes (33E/34E family) and evaporator fan codes account for most service visits.

models.name: Samsung NE59M4320SS Electric Range
models.notes: Freestanding 5.9 cu ft electric range. The E-08 (oven not heating) and SE (stuck key) codes are the two most common; SE usually means a shorted membrane keypad.

models.name: Samsung NX58R5601SS Gas Range
models.notes: 5.8 cu ft gas range with illuminated knobs. Temperature-sensor codes dominate; the oven sensor is an inexpensive DIY part on this model.

models.name: Samsung NE63A6511SS Electric Range
models.notes: 6.3 cu ft electric range with Air Fry. Uses the current Samsung E-code set; codes display on the front control panel.

models.name: Samsung ME19R7041FS Over-the-Range Microwave
models.notes: Best-selling OTR microwave. The infamous SE (stuck key) code on this platform is almost always a failed membrane keypad — Samsung extended warranties for it on some serials.

models.name: LG WM3400CW Front-Load Washer
models.notes: Entry-level 4.5 cu ft front-loader. Shares the full LG two-letter code set; drain (OE) and inlet (IE) codes are the most common service triggers.

models.name: LG WT7300CW Top-Load Washer
models.notes: 5.0 cu ft top loader with TurboWash3D. UE unbalance codes lead complaints on this platform — bulky single items are the usual cause.

models.name: LG WM8100HVA Front-Load Washer
models.notes: 5.2 cu ft mega-capacity front-loader. Direct-drive motor codes (LE) and drain codes (OE) are the most frequent; the drain pump filter is front-accessible.

models.name: LG WM4500HBA Front-Load Washer
models.notes: 5.0 cu ft WashTower-adjacent front-loader with AI DD 2.0. Code set carries over from the WM4000; drain and door codes dominate.

models.name: LG WT7900HBA Top-Load Washer
models.notes: 5.5 cu ft top loader with TurboWash3D and EasyUnload. Standard LG top-load code set; unbalance and slow-drain codes are the usual suspects.

models.name: LG DLE3500W Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7.4 cu ft electric dryer. The D80/D90 airflow codes are by far the most common — they measure duct restriction and almost always indicate lint buildup, not a failed part.

models.name: LG DLEX4500B Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7.4 cu ft TurboSteam dryer in matte black. Same airflow-code family as the DLEX3900; nF (no flow) joins D80/D90/D95 on this generation.

models.name: LG DLE7150W Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7.4 cu ft sensor-dry model. nP (no power to heater circuit) usually means a tripped breaker leg on 240V models — check the panel before calling service.

models.name: LG DLG3601W Gas Dryer
models.notes: 7.4 cu ft gas dryer. The GAS code points to supply or igniter problems; airflow codes behave identically to the electric models.

models.name: LG LDFN4542S Dishwasher
models.notes: LG's volume front-control dishwasher. The AE leak code and OE drain code lead service data; the leak sensor sits in the base pan.

models.name: LG LDTS5552S Top-Control Dishwasher
models.notes: QuadWash Pro top-control model with TrueSteam. Same code family as the LDFN series; FE (overfill) often follows a stuck inlet valve.

models.name: LG LDF5545ST Dishwasher
models.notes: Front-control QuadWash workhorse. Drain (OE) and inlet (IE) codes are the most common; the drain pump is accessible without pulling the unit.

models.name: LG LFXS28968S French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 28 cu ft French door model from the linear-compressor era. Fan codes (Er rF/Er FF) often trace to evaporator icing caused by a weak defrost cycle.

models.name: LG LRFVS3006S InstaView French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 30 cu ft InstaView Door-in-Door flagship. Craft Ice and dual ice-maker hardware add the IF code family to the standard LG fridge code set.

models.name: LG LMXS28626S 4-Door French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 26 cu ft 4-door model. The Er dH defrost-heater code is the signature failure of this generation; the freezer sensor (Er FS) is a close second.

models.name: LG LRFDS3016S French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 30 cu ft standard-depth French door with dual ice makers. Codes display on the control panel inside the left door; fan and ice-maker codes dominate.

models.name: LG LREL6325F Electric Range
models.notes: 6.3 cu ft electric range with Air Fry and EasyClean. The F9 (oven not heating) code is the platform's most-searched fault; check the bake element first.

models.name: LG LRGL5825F Gas Range
models.notes: 5.8 cu ft gas range with Air Fry. F9 on gas models usually points to the igniter rather than the control board — a glowing-but-weak igniter won't open the gas valve.

models.name: Whirlpool WTW4816FW Top-Load Washer
models.notes: 3.5 cu ft budget VMW top loader. Lid-lock (F5E2) and shifter (F7E1) codes are the platform's defining faults; both are well-documented DIY repairs.

models.name: Whirlpool WTW8127LC Top-Load Washer
models.notes: 5.2–5.3 cu ft top loader with removable agitator and 2-in-1 flex dispenser. Current-generation VMW platform; same F#E# code set as earlier models.

models.name: Whirlpool WFW9620HC Front-Load Washer
models.notes: 5.0 cu ft flagship front-loader with Load & Go XL. Drain-time (F9E1) and door-lock codes lead service data; the drain filter is behind the lower front panel.

models.name: Whirlpool WTW5057LW Top-Load Washer
models.notes: 4.7–4.8 cu ft top loader with removable agitator. Uses the standard VMW diagnostic set; lid-lock errors after power blips are common and usually clear with a reset.

models.name: Whirlpool WFW6620HW Front-Load Washer
models.notes: 4.5 cu ft closet-depth front-loader. The F9E1 long-drain code is the top complaint — coins and debris in the drain pump filter are the usual cause.

models.name: Whirlpool WED5620HW Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7.4 cu ft electric dryer with steam. The AF (airflow) code is a duct-restriction warning; F01 points to the main control board.

models.name: Whirlpool WED6620HW Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7.4 cu ft model with Load & Go-matching styling. Moisture-sensor (F28) and thermistor (F22) codes are the most common after airflow warnings.

models.name: Whirlpool WGD4815EW Gas Dryer
models.notes: Gas twin of the WED4815EW. Thermal fuse and igniter failures present as heating codes; the fuse blows protectively when the vent is restricted.

models.name: Whirlpool WDT730PAHZ Dishwasher
models.notes: Top-control model with third rack. F9E1 (drain) and F6E4 (float switch) are the leading codes; both display as flash patterns on models without a digit display.

models.name: Whirlpool WDTA50SAKZ Dishwasher
models.notes: Large-capacity top-control model. Same F#E# diagnostic pairs as the rest of the platform; drain codes lead service data.

models.name: Whirlpool WDF330PAHS Dishwasher
models.notes: Budget front-control model with the Clean-light blink-code interface. Count the blinks to read the F and E values, then look up the pair.

models.name: Whirlpool WRX735SDHZ French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 25 cu ft 4-door model. PO (power outage) is informational; the SY EF evaporator-fan code is the one that takes out cooling and deserves immediate attention.

models.name: Whirlpool WRF555SDFZ French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 25 cu ft French door with interior water dispenser. Dispenser-panel codes follow the Whirlpool side-by-side convention; PO and CF are the most seen.

models.name: Whirlpool WRT318FZDW Top-Freezer Refrigerator
models.notes: 18 cu ft top-freezer workhorse. No digital display — faults present as temperature drift rather than codes; the electronic control board reports codes only in service mode.

models.name: Whirlpool WFE525S0JZ Electric Range
models.notes: 5.3 cu ft freestanding electric range. F3-E0 (open oven sensor) is the most common code; the sensor is a 15-minute DIY replacement.

models.name: Whirlpool WFG320M0BS Gas Range
models.notes: 5.1 cu ft freestanding gas range. Keypad codes (F2-E0/E1) usually mean moisture or heat damage to the membrane switch above the oven vent.

models.name: GE GFW550SSNWW Front-Load Washer
models.notes: 4.8 cu ft front-loader with UltraFresh vent system. GE's E-series codes display on the front panel; drain (E64 family) codes are the most common.

models.name: GE GFW850SPNRS Front-Load Washer
models.notes: 5.0 cu ft flagship with SmartDispense. Dispense-system and drain codes lead service data; the bulk dispenser adds its own fault family.

models.name: GE GFW650SSNWW Front-Load Washer
models.notes: 4.8 cu ft model one trim above the GFW550 with steam. Shares the identical E-code diagnostic set.

models.name: GE GTD42EASJWW Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7.2 cu ft aluminized-alloy drum dryer. Long-running budget model; heating-circuit codes (E1/E3/E4 family) are the usual service triggers.

models.name: GE GFD55ESSNWW Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7.8 cu ft front-load matching dryer. The LS (lint screen) reminder is informational; E-series codes indicate real sensor or heater faults.

models.name: GE GDT665SSNSS Dishwasher
models.notes: Top-control model with bottle jets. GE's C-codes cover the wash system: C4 is the drain fault most owners meet first.

models.name: GE GDP630PYRFS Dishwasher
models.notes: Front-control Dry Boost model. The FL (flood/leak) code and HO (heating delay) status are the two most-searched on this platform.

models.name: GE GSS25GYPFS Side-by-Side Refrigerator
models.notes: 25.3 cu ft side-by-side. Codes show on the dispenser display; dS (defrost sensor) and the 88 display test are common sights after power interruptions.

models.name: GE GFE26JYMFS French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 25.6 cu ft French door model. The turbo-cool-era control platform reports E-codes via the temperature display; defrost faults dominate.

models.name: GE Profile PVD28BYNFS 4-Door French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 27.9 cu ft Profile flagship with door-in-door. Same diagnostic family as mainline GE fridges plus connectivity status codes.

models.name: GE JB645RKSS Electric Range
models.notes: 5.3 cu ft freestanding electric range — a perennial best-seller. F2 (over-temp) and F3/F4 (sensor) codes are the classic GE oven faults.

models.name: GE JGB735SPSS Gas Range
models.notes: 5.0 cu ft gas range with air fry. The F-code set carries over from GE's electric platform; F7 (stuck/shorted keypad) often appears after cleaning the control panel.

models.name: Bosch SHXM4AY55N 100 Series Dishwasher
models.notes: Entry-level Bosch with a plastic tub. Same E-code diagnostics as the 300/500/800 Series — E15 base-pan water and E24/E25 drain faults lead the list.

models.name: Bosch SHEM3AY55N 100 Series Dishwasher
models.notes: Front-handle 100 Series variant. The E25 drain code on this platform usually means a blocked impeller cover — a 5-minute fix without tools.

models.name: Bosch SHP878ZD5N 800 Series Dishwasher
models.notes: 800 Series with CrystalDry. Premium trim, identical diagnostic set; E01 control-board codes are rarer but more expensive when they appear.

models.name: Bosch SHX878ZD5N 800 Series Dishwasher
models.notes: Bar-handle 800 Series CrystalDry model. E15 remains the signature Bosch code — tip the unit 45° forward to drain the base pan and it usually clears.

models.name: Bosch WAT28400UC 300 Series Compact Washer
models.notes: 24-inch compact front-loader, ubiquitous in condos. E18 (drain) and E17 (inlet) are the dominant codes; the drain pump filter is behind the lower right panel.

models.name: Bosch WGA12400UC 500 Series Compact Washer
models.notes: Current-generation 24-inch compact washer. Carries the same E-code set as the 300 Series it replaced.

models.name: Bosch HBL8453UC 800 Series Wall Oven
models.notes: 30-inch single wall oven. E-numbered codes display on the control panel; the E132 safety cutout demands immediate attention.

models.name: Maytag MVW7230HW Top-Load Washer
models.notes: 5.2 cu ft top loader with Extra Power button. Whirlpool VMW platform underneath; F8E1 fill faults and SUD detection are the most-searched codes.

models.name: Maytag MHW6630HW Front-Load Washer
models.notes: 4.8 cu ft front-loader with 16-hr Fresh Hold. F9E1 slow-drain is the leading code — the drain filter behind the lower panel collects everything.

models.name: Maytag MVW4505MW Top-Load Washer
models.notes: 4.5 cu ft deep-fill top loader. Current VMW platform with the standard F#E# code set; lid-lock faults clear with a power reset more often than not.

models.name: Maytag MED7230HW Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7.4 cu ft dryer with Extra Power. F01 main-board and F22 thermistor codes are the platform's most common; check the vent before replacing sensors.

models.name: Maytag MED4500MW Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7.0 cu ft Pet Pro-adjacent platform. Shares the Whirlpool dryer code set; F06 motor-circuit codes are rare but terminal for the drive motor.

models.name: Maytag MER8800FZ Electric Range
models.notes: 6.4 cu ft electric range. Whirlpool oven platform — F-E paired codes; F3-E0 oven sensor faults are the most common and cheapest to fix.

models.name: Kenmore 665-Series Dishwashers (Whirlpool-built)
models.notes: Model numbers starting with 665. are Whirlpool-built Kenmore dishwashers. They use Whirlpool's F#E# diagnostic pairs, read as blink patterns on the Clean light.

models.name: Kenmore 795-Series Refrigerators (LG-built)
models.notes: Model numbers starting with 795. are LG-built Kenmore (mostly Elite) refrigerators. They use LG's ER-prefix code set on the display panel.

models.name: Kenmore 110-Series Dryers (Whirlpool-built)
models.notes: 110-prefix Kenmore dryers are Whirlpool-built and use the F01/F22/F28 code family. The AF airflow warning means check the duct run first.

models.name: Kenmore Elite HE3 & HE4 Washers (Whirlpool-built)
models.notes: The classic Whirlpool Duet platform sold under the Kenmore Elite badge. The F21 slow-drain code is the defining fault — the coin-trap filter is almost always the culprit.

models.name: Kenmore 790-Series Ranges (Frigidaire-built)
models.notes: 790-prefix Kenmore ranges are Frigidaire-built and use Frigidaire's F10-series codes. The F10 runaway-temperature code should never be ignored.

models.name: Frigidaire FFSS2615TS Side-by-Side Refrigerator
models.notes: 25.5 cu ft side-by-side. The H1 high-temperature code and SY EF evaporator-fan code are the two faults that take out cooling on this platform.

models.name: Frigidaire Gallery FGHB2868TF French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 27.6 cu ft Gallery French door model. Codes display on the front panel; fan and high-temp alerts dominate the service history.

models.name: Frigidaire Gallery FGID2466QF Dishwasher
models.notes: Gallery top-control model. Frigidaire's i-codes: i20 (clogged filter/drain) is by far the most common — clean the fine filter and check the air gap.

models.name: Frigidaire FFBD2412SS 24-inch Dishwasher
models.notes: Long-running budget dishwasher. The i30 base-pan leak code stops everything until the pan dries out; PF after storms is informational.

models.name: Frigidaire FCRE3052AS Electric Range
models.notes: 5.3 cu ft electric range. The F10 runaway-temp and F30/F31 sensor codes carry over from the long-running Frigidaire range platform.

models.name: Frigidaire Gallery FGGH3047VF Gas Range
models.notes: 30-inch front-control gas range with air fry. F90-series door-lock codes appear most often after self-clean cycles.

models.name: Frigidaire FFRE4120SW Electric Dryer
models.notes: 6.7 cu ft budget dryer. E64 heating-element failure is the platform's signature fault; the element is an inexpensive DIY part.

models.name: Electrolux ELFW7637AT Front-Load Washer
models.notes: 4.5 cu ft front-loader with SmartBoost premix. Current-generation E-code set; E20 drain restrictions lead service data.

models.name: Electrolux EFME627UTT Electric Dryer
models.notes: 8.0 cu ft dryer matching the EFLS627 washer. E61 heating-relay and E64 element codes are the most common; both point to the heater circuit.

models.name: Electrolux ELFE7637AT Electric Dryer
models.notes: 8.0 cu ft balanced-dry dryer. Shares the E6x heater-circuit code family with its predecessor.

models.name: Electrolux EI24ID30QS Dishwasher
models.notes: 24-inch IQ-Touch dishwasher. Electrolux i-codes mirror Frigidaire's (same parent company): i20 drain restriction is the volume leader.

models.name: KitchenAid KDTM404KPS Dishwasher
models.notes: Top-control model with FreeFlex third rack. Whirlpool platform — codes read as blink-pattern pairs; 7-1 heater faults stop the dry cycle.

models.name: KitchenAid KDFE104HPS Dishwasher
models.notes: Front-control 46 dBA model. The 6-2 inlet-valve and 7-1 heater codes are the most common; both parts are shared with Whirlpool equivalents.

models.name: KitchenAid KRFF507HPS French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 26.8 cu ft French door model. Whirlpool fridge platform — SY EF evaporator-fan faults are the code to act on quickly.

models.name: KitchenAid KRMF706ESS 5-Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 25.8 cu ft multi-door flagship. Codes show on the dispenser panel; fan and communication faults dominate its service record.

models.name: KitchenAid KFEG500ESS Electric Range
models.notes: 5.8 cu ft freestanding electric range. Whirlpool oven platform with F#E# pairs; keypad codes (F2E0/F2E1) usually follow control-panel moisture.

models.name: KitchenAid KOSE500ESS Single Wall Oven
models.notes: 30-inch Even-Heat wall oven. F6E0 communication and F9E0 door-latch codes are the platform's most-searched faults.

models.name: KitchenAid KSGG700ESS Gas Range
models.notes: 5.8 cu ft gas range with even-heat convection. Same Whirlpool F-E code pairs as the electric models.

models.name: Miele W1 Series Compact Washers (WWB/WWH/WXD)
models.notes: Miele's 24-inch W1 platform. F-numbered codes display on the panel; F11 drainage faults and F53 speed-sensor codes are the most commonly searched.

models.name: Miele G 7000 Series Dishwashers
models.notes: Current AutoDos generation. F11 (drain) and F13 (water intake) carry over from earlier G-series platforms; F70 float-switch faults need the base pan dried.

models.name: Miele T1 Series Heat-Pump Dryers
models.notes: Ventless heat-pump dryers. F66 heater-relay and F53 sensor codes are rare but documented; most T1 complaints are airflow maintenance, not faults.

models.name: Speed Queen TR3003WN Top-Load Washer
models.notes: Commercial-grade 3.2 cu ft top loader with mechanical-feel controls. Electronic control underneath reports E-codes; most faults are fill or drain related.

models.name: Speed Queen TR7003WN Top-Load Washer
models.notes: Flagship top loader with 7-year warranty. Same TR-platform diagnostics as the TR3; errors are uncommon enough that most owners never see one.

models.name: Speed Queen FF7005WN Front-Load Washer
models.notes: 3.5 cu ft commercial-grade front-loader. E-codes display on the electronic panel; door-lock and drain faults are the most reported.

models.name: Speed Queen DR3003WE Electric Dryer
models.notes: 7.0 cu ft metal-cabinet dryer. Minimal electronics mean few codes; E1/E3 sensor faults are the main entries in its diagnostic table.

models.name: Amana NTW4516FW Top-Load Washer
models.notes: 3.5 cu ft budget top loader on the Whirlpool VMW platform. F8E1 fill faults and SUD over-sudsing are the codes owners search most.

models.name: Amana NED4655EW Electric Dryer
models.notes: 6.5 cu ft budget dryer. Whirlpool platform; F01 control-board and F22 thermistor codes cover most of its diagnostic table.

models.name: Amana ART318FFDS Top-Freezer Refrigerator
models.notes: 18 cu ft top-freezer. The H1 high-temperature alert is the only code most owners ever encounter — usually after a door left ajar.

models.name: Amana AER6303MFS Electric Range
models.notes: 4.8 cu ft electric range. Whirlpool oven platform with F#E# codes; F1E0 board faults and F2E0 keypad shorts lead the list.

models.name: Haier HLC1700AXW Washer-Dryer Combo
models.notes: 24-inch ventless all-in-one, common in apartments. E-codes cover both wash and dry functions; drain (E1/E2 family) faults dominate.

models.name: Haier HRQ16N3BGS Quad-Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 16.4 cu ft counter-depth quad-door. F-codes show on the internal display; sensor faults (F1/F3) are the most reported.

models.name: Fisher & Paykel DD24 DishDrawer Series
models.notes: The iconic two-drawer dishwasher. U-codes are user-clearable faults: U1 (fill) and U4 (drain) account for the majority of displays.

models.name: Fisher & Paykel WH2424P1 Front-Load Washer
models.notes: 24-inch compact front-loader. Numeric fault codes display on the panel; most are sensor or fill related and clear with a power cycle.

models.name: Fisher & Paykel RF170ADUSX4 ActiveSmart French Door Refrigerator
models.notes: 17.1 cu ft counter-depth French door. ActiveSmart control reports F-codes via display flashes; fan faults (F1/F2) are the main entries.
